title: fix: enable imageSize parameter for nanobanana-2
author: pollinations-ai[bot]

gist.headline: Stretching the canvas
gist.blurb: Our nanobanana-2 model was stubbornly ignoring your size requests and painting everything in 1024x1024. We've fixed the dimensions and added a snappy new 512x512 tier.
gist.summary: Enabled the imageSize parameter for the nanobanana-2 model and introduced a new 0.5K resolution tier.
gist.impact: Custom width and height requests now work correctly for nanobanana-2, allowing for both smaller (512x512) and larger (2K) image generations.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot adjusting the size of a digital canvas. The robot holds a tiny frame (representing 0.5K) while the bee works on a large one (2K). Subtle banana motif for 'nanobanana'.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a chunky monitor-head robot stretching a glowing digital canvas to different sizes. The robot holds a tiny square picture frame while the bee paints on a massive canvas. A small pixelated banana sits on a wooden desk nearby. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel peach gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, chunky sprites.
